title: Quantitative Immunoblotting Analysis of LRRK2 Signalling Pathway
author: Francesca Tonelli

steps.title: Please choose the relevant method for preparation of lysate
steps.instruction: Please choose the relevant method for preparation of lysate: Preparation of lysates from cultured cells Preparation of lysates from mouse tissues

steps.title: Quickly wash cells in the tissue...
steps.instruction: Quickly wash cells in the tissue culture dish by carefully pouring Room temperature, culture media without Foetal bovine serum (FBS) that the cells are currently growing in into the dish. We do not recommend using Phosphate Buffered Saline to wash cells prior to cell lysis as this may cause nutrient stress.

steps.title: Pour off media without FBS from...
steps.instruction: Pour off media without FBS from the culture dish, tilt dish and completely aspirate all residual media. Immediately add freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer, ensuring that the entire surface is covered by lysis buffer. The amount of lysis buffer to use will depend on cell type and cell confluency. As a guideline, use 0.1-0.2 ml of lysis buffer for each well of a 6 well plate, 0.5 ml for a 10 cm dish and 1 ml for a 15 cm dish. It is preferable to aim for protein concentrations of a least 0.75 µg/µl as this will enable the appropriate amount of protein to be loaded onto a gel as detailed below.

steps.title: Immediately place the cell dishes On ice
steps.instruction: Immediately place the cell dishes On ice .

steps.title: Scrape the cells on the dish...
steps.instruction: Scrape the cells on the dish using a cell lifter (Sigma-Aldrich CLS3008, or equivalent) to ensure all cells are detached from the dish.

steps.title: Using a pipette, transfer cell lysate...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette, transfer cell lysate to an Eppendorf tube On ice . For non-adherent cells, transfer cells to a Falcon tube and pellet by centrifugation at 180 x g, °C, 3 minutes ; wash cells once with Room temperature, culture media without FBS and pellet again as above. Discard supernatant and add freshly prepared ice-cold lysis buffer. Immediately transfer the Falcon containing the cell pellet to ice.

steps.title: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00...
steps.instruction: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00 to allow for efficient lysis.

steps.title: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800...
steps.instruction: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf tubes and discard the pellet. Keep the tubes On ice . Cell lysates can be snap frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80 °C for future use.

steps.title: Determine the protein concentration of cell...
steps.instruction: Determine the protein concentration of cell lysates by Bradford assay according to the manufacturer’s instructions, performing measurements in triplicate. Ensure the concentration of the samples is in the linear range for the Bradford assay. If it isn’t, prepare appropriate dilutions in water of each lysate. Generally, protein concentrations of near confluent cells lysed as described above should range from 0.5 to 5 µg/µl (depending on cell type).

steps.title: To ensure the specificity of the...
steps.instruction: To ensure the specificity of the phosphorylation signals detected by immunoblotting in cell lysates, we recommend treating cells for at least 30 min ± a LRRK2 kinase inhibitor such as MLi-2 at 100nM final concentration (or equivalent volume of DMSO vehicle) before lysis.

steps.title: To ensure the specificity of the...
steps.instruction: To ensure the specificity of the phosphorylation signals detected by immunoblotting in tissue lysates, we recommend administering mice with MLI-2 (30 mg/kg final dose) by subcutaneous injection for 2 hours prior to tissue collection.

steps.title: Collect tissues from mice culled by...
steps.instruction: Collect tissues from mice culled by cervical dislocation and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen immediately after harvesting. Store at -80 °C until further use.

steps.title: Ground frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Ground frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen to a fine powder using a pulveriser kit.

steps.title: Using a spatula, transfer the tissue...
steps.instruction: Using a spatula, transfer the tissue powder to an Eppendorf tube on dry ice. Maintain all Pulverizer units in liquid nitrogen including spatula. After each use wash the pulveriser with tap water and clean thoroughly with 70% (v/v) ethanol.

steps.title: Weigh tissue powder
steps.instruction: Weigh tissue powder. Keep samples on dry ice during weighing.

steps.title: Immediately add a 10-fold volume excess...
steps.instruction: Immediately add a 10-fold volume excess of ice-cold lysis buffer to the tissue powder (for example, add 1000 μl of lysis buffer to 100 mg of tissue powder) and quickly vortex to mix.

steps.title: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00...
steps.instruction: Leave samples On ice for 00:20:00 to allow for efficient lysis.

steps.title: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800...
steps.instruction: Clarify lysates by centrifugation at 20800 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants into new Eppendorf tubes and discard the pellet.

steps.title: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve...
steps.instruction: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve the same protein concentration for all samples (ideally, 0.5-2 µg/µl, depending on the sample at the lowest concentration) by combining the cell lysate with lysis buffer. Add a quarter of a volume of 4X SDS/LDS loading buffer freshly supplemented with beta-mercaptoethanol (i.e. for 7.5 µl of lysate/lysis buffer mix, add 2.5 µl of loading buffer). Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70 °C heating block before immunoblot analysis.

steps.title: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12%...
steps.instruction: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12% Bis–Tris Midi Gel (ThermoFisherScientific, Cat#WG1402BOX or Cat#WG1403BOX), or a self-cast 10% Bis-Tris gel, alongside pre-stained molecular weight markers (ranging from 10 kDa to 250 kDa). Rinse wells carefully with running buffer before loading samples. The amount of protein loaded for each sample ranges from 10 to 40 μg, depending on the cell type and the protein(s) of interest. For cell lines like mouse embryonic fibroblasts, A549 cells and cells isolated from human peripheral blood (monocytes, neutrophils), we recommend loading 10-15 μg of protein for each cell extract for optimal signal. Be aware of maximum loading capacity of each well as per manufacturer’s instructions and take care not to overload wells. If multiple gels are used for each set of experimental samples, an internal loading control should also be included for subsequent data normalization.

steps.title: Electrophorese samples at 130V with MOPS...
steps.instruction: Electrophorese samples at 130V with MOPS SDS running buffer for 02:00:00 or until the blue dye runs off the gel.

steps.title: Equilibrate the gel, one piece of...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the gel, one piece of nitrocellulose membrane (GE Healthcare, Amersham Protran Supported 0.45 μm NC) and two pieces of filter paper (WhatmanTM 3MM Chr Chromatography Paper, or equivalent) (all of the same size as the gel) by pre-soaking them in transfer buffer.

steps.title: Assemble the gel and membrane transfer...
steps.instruction: Assemble the gel and membrane transfer stack in a tray filled with transfer buffer to ensure that all components are submerged during the assembling. Place one sponge pad inside the cassette holder (on the side that will be facing the cathode). Place one piece of filter paper on top of the sponge pad, followed by the gel, nitrocellulose membrane, another piece of filter paper and another sponge pad. Carefully remove any air bubbles between layers using a roller after adding each layer.

steps.title: Carefully close the cassette holder and...
steps.instruction: Carefully close the cassette holder and insert it in the transfer tank. Fill the tank with transfer buffer.

steps.title: Electrophoretically transfer proteins from gel onto...
steps.instruction: Electrophoretically transfer proteins from gel onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V (constant voltage) for 01:40:00 On ice using a wet transfer system.

steps.title: After transfer, stain membranes with Ponceau...
steps.instruction: After transfer, stain membranes with Ponceau solution to assess transfer efficiency and general quality of the samples. If an image is required for record, the Ponceau-stained membraned can be scanned.

steps.title: Each membrane can be divided into...
steps.instruction: Each membrane can be divided into three sections by two horizontal cuts (one cut just above the 75 kDa ladder band and another cut just below the 37.5 kDa ladder band): ‘top section’ (from the top of the membrane to the 75 kDa marker), ‘middle section’ (between the 75 kDa and the 37.5 kDa marker), and ‘bottom section’ (from the 37.5 kDa marker to the bottom of the membrane) (Figure 1), to be incubated with primary antibodies as described in Step 23. Samples loaded onto one gel can be simultaneously blotted for LRRK2-total and LRRK2-pSer935, Rab10-total and Rab10-pThr73 and a loading control like alpha tubulin or glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH). If in addition Rab12-total and Rab12-pSer105 levels are analysed, duplicate gels are required. Figure 1: After protein transfer, each membrane can be divided into three sections as shown here: ‘top section’ (to be blotted for LRRK2-total and LRRK2-pSer935), ‘middle section’ (to be blotted for a loading control like alpha tubulin or GAPDH), and ‘bottom section’ (to be blotted for Rab10-total and Rab10-pThr73, or Rab12-total and Rab12-pSer105).

steps.title: Destain membranes from Step 21 by...
steps.instruction: Destain membranes from Step 21 by washing with TBS-T and incubate in blocking solution for at least 00:15:00 at room temperature on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: Rinse the membrane in TBS-T and...
steps.instruction: Rinse the membrane in TBS-T and incubate Overnight at 4 °C with primary antibodies (diluted in 5% (w/v) BSA in TBS-T to their working concentration – Table 1), as follows: ‘top section’: incubate with anti-total and phospho-LRRK2 antibodies; ‘middle section’: incubate with anti-alpha tubulin or GAPDH antibody; ‘bottom section’: incubate with anti-total and phospho-Rab antibodies. If assessing protein phosphorylation, we multiplex the phospho-specific antibody with the total antibody detecting the protein of interest. Table 1 lists the primary antibodies most commonly used in our lab to study LRRK2 signalling and their suggested working dilution. The selectivity and specificity of the antibodies suggested in Table 1 have been extensively validated using appropriate controls, including overexpression models and knock-out cell lines. All antibodies listed in Table 1 react with both human and mouse cells/tissues. A B C D E 1 Antibody Target Company Cat. number Host species Dilution 2 Rab10 pThr73 Abcam Inc. ab230261 Rabbit 1 µg/ml 3 Rab10 (total) Nanotools 0680-100/Rab10-605B11 Mouse 1 µg/ml 4 Rab10 (total) Abcam Inc. ab237703 Rabbit 1 µg/ml 5 Rab12 pSer105 (mouse) /pSer106 (human) Abcam Inc. ab256487 Rabbit 1 µg/ml 6 Rab12 (total) MRC-PPU Reagents andServices, University ofDundee SA227 Sheep 1 µg/ml 7 Rab12 (total) Proteintech 18843-1-AP Rabbit 1 µg/ml 8 LRRK2 pSer935 MRC-PPU Reagents andServices, University ofDundee UDD2 Rabbit 1 µg/ml 9 LRRK2 (total) (C-terminus) Antibodies Inc./NeuroMab 75-253 Mouse 1 µg/ml 10 LRRK2 (total) (N-terminus) MRC-PPU Reagents andServices, University ofDundee UDD3 Rabbit 0.1 µg/ml 11 GAPDH Antibodies Inc./NeuroMab sc-32233 Mouse 1:5,000 12 alpha-tubulin Cell Signaling Technology 3873 Mouse 1:5,000 Table 1.

steps.title: After incubation with primary antibodies, wash...
steps.instruction: After incubation with primary antibodies, wash membranes in TBS-T (3 washes, 5-10 minutes each, on a see-saw rocker):

steps.title: (Wash 1/3)
steps.instruction: (Wash 1/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: (Wash 2/3)
steps.instruction: (Wash 2/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: (Wash 3/3)
steps.instruction: (Wash 3/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: Incubate membranes with near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled...
steps.instruction: Incubate membranes with near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled secondary antibodies (diluted to the working concentration: 1:20,000) for 01:00:00 at Room temperature on a see-saw rocker. If multiplexing primary antibodies, use secondary antibodies labelled with spectrally distinct nearinfrared fluorescent dyes. Generally, we use IRDye 800CW (800 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the phospho-antibodies multiplexed with IRDye 680LT (680 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the corresponding total antibody. Table 2 lists the near-infrared fluorescent dye-labelled secondary antibodies used in our lab. A B C D 1 Secondary Antibodies Company Cat. number Notes 2 goat anti-mouse IRDye 680LT LI-COR 926-68020 3 goat anti-mouse IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32210 4 goat anti-rabbit IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32211 5 donkey anti-mouse IRDye 680LT LI-COR 926-68022 6 donkey anti-mouse IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32212 7 donkey anti-rabbit IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32213 8 donkey anti-goat IRDye 800CW LI-COR 926-32214 Reacts with Sheep primary Abs Table 2.

steps.title: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T (4...
steps.instruction: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T (4 washes, 10-15 minutes each, with agitation):

steps.title: (Wash 1/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 1/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 2/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 2/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 3/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 3/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 4/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 4/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: Protein bands are acquired via near...
steps.instruction: Protein bands are acquired via near infrared fluorescent detection using the Odyssey CLx Imaging System and the signal intensity quantified using the Image Studio Software. To control for inter-gel variability, the signal intensity of each band can be normalised against the control sample loaded in each gel of a set of experiments.

steps.title: Analyse immunoblotting data using a software...
steps.instruction: Analyse immunoblotting data using a software for statistical analysis (Graphpad Prism, or equivalent).

steps.title: Determine the protein concentration of tissue...
steps.instruction: Determine the protein concentration of tissue lysates by Bradford assay according to the manufacturer’s instructions, performing measurements in triplicate. Ensure the concentration of the samples is in the linear range for the Bradford assay. If it isn’t, prepare appropriate dilutions in water of each lysate. Generally, tissues lysed as described above should result in protein concentrations of at least 2 µg/µl.

steps.title: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve...
steps.instruction: Prepare samples for immunoblotting to achieve the same protein concentration for all samples (ideally, 0.5-2 µg/µl, depending on the sample at the lowest concentration) by combining the lysate with lysis buffer. Add a quarter of a volume of 4X SDS/LDS loading buffer freshly supplemented with beta-mercaptoethanol (i.e. for 7.5 µl of lysate/lysis buffer mix, add 2.5 µl of loading buffer). Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 00:05:00 at 70 °C heating block before immunoblot analysis.

steps.title: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12%...
steps.instruction: Load samples onto a NuPAGE 4–12% Bis–Tris Midi Gel (ThermoFisherScientific, Cat#WG1402BOX or Cat#WG1403BOX), or a self-cast 10% Bis-Tris gel, alongside pre-stained molecular weight markers (ranging from 10 kDa to 250 kDa). Rinse wells carefully with running buffer before loading samples. The amount of protein loaded for each sample ranges from 10 to 40 μg, depending on the tissue type and the protein(s) of interest. For mouse tissues like brain, spleen, kidney, lung and intestine we recommend loading 30-40 μg of protein for each tissue extract for optimal signal. Be aware of maximum loading capacity of each well as per manufacturer’s instructions and take care not to overload wells. If multiple gels are used for each set of experimental samples, an internal loading control should also be included for subsequent data normalization.

steps.instruction: Electrophorese samples at 130V with MOPS SDS running buffer for 02:00:00 or until the blue dye runs off the gel.

Table 1 lists the primary antibodies most commonly used in our lab to study LRRK2 signalling and their suggested working dilution. steps.instruction: After incubation with primary antibodies, wash membranes in TBS-T (3 washes, 5-10 minutes each, on a see-saw rocker):

steps.title: (Wash 1/3)
steps.instruction: (Wash 1/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

steps.title: (Wash 2/3)
steps.instruction: (Wash 2/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

steps.instruction: (Wash 3/3): Wash membranes in TBS-T 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , on a see-saw rocker.

Generally, we use IRDye 800CW (800 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the phospho-antibodies multiplexed with IRDye 680LT (680 nm channel) secondary antibodies for the corresponding total antibody. steps.title: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T (4...
steps.instruction: Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T (4 washes, 10-15 minutes each, with agitation):

steps.title: (Wash 1/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 1/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 2/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 2/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 3/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 3/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

steps.title: (Wash 4/4)
steps.instruction: (Wash 4/4): Extensively wash membranes in TBS-T 00:10:00 - 00:15:00 , with agitation.

To control for inter-gel variability, the signal intensity of each band can be normalised against the control sample loaded in each gel of a set of experiments.
